does: This paper establishes foundational concepts and frameworks for mechanistic interpretability research by clarifying the ultimate goals and aspirations of the field. It examines what practitioners are fundamentally trying to achieve when they study neural network internals—understanding how models compute, what internal structures represent, and how information flows through layers. The work articulates key desiderata for interpretability methods, such as compositionality, causal understanding, and actionable insights into model behavior. By grounding mechanistic interpretability in explicit end-goals rather than treating it as a purely technical exercise, the paper provides conceptual scaffolding for evaluating which research directions matter and why.
connects: This work responds to the rapid expansion of mechanistic interpretability techniques—including circuit analysis, neuron probing, attention head analysis, and feature attribution methods—by asking what problem these tools are actually solving. It builds on foundational concerns in explainability and transparency literature about whether interpretability methods produce genuine understanding or merely post-hoc rationalization. The paper engages with ongoing debates about what constitutes a valid explanation in machine learning and draws from philosophy of science and cognitive science discussions of mechanistic explanation. It extends prior work questioning whether interpretability should aim for human-understandable descriptions or formal mathematical specifications.
enables: By clarifying the goals of mechanistic interpretability, this work enables more strategic research prioritization and method development in the field. It positions future work to design interpretability techniques that target specific, well-defined objectives rather than interpretability-as-a-general-good. The framework supports the development of benchmarks for evaluating whether interpretability methods genuinely achieve their stated purposes. This foundation could facilitate connections between mechanistic interpretability and downstream applications like model editing, robustness improvement, and AI safety—by making explicit how understanding mechanisms should translate into practical capabilities. It also opens space for interdisciplinary collaboration by clarifying where interpretability intersects with philosophy, cognitive science, and other fields studying explanation.
